figures.caption: Figure 4: Representative developed physical fields from the mixed corpus. Left: velocity magnitude from an obstacle-rich V2 continuousflow Zarr store. Right: velocity magnitude from a porous-media permeability sample extracted from the original V3 Zarr store. Spatial slices are shown for both domains.
Figure 3: Convergence curves. The total objective and latent reconstruction loss decline over training. Final held-out validation markers show both domains converging near 10−5 latent MSE.

Figure 1: Shodh-MoE architecture. A physics-informed tokenizer maps 1283 physical fields to structured 163 latents. A sparse transport block combines shared experts for universal symmetries with routed experts selected by a Top-1 soft-semantic router. The decoder maps transported latents back to physical space.

Figure 2: Routing bifurcation over training. The router progressively separates the two physical regimes, sending open-channel Navier– Stokes-type tokens to Expert 0 and porous-media Darcy–Brinkman– Stokes-type tokens to Expert 1. Curves show smoothed token fractions computed from raw routing telemetry.
